metadata.title: The Color Accessibility Check (WebAIM), Step by Step
metadata.playlist: Ship It — Build Your Brand's Live Presence
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 8 brief — Building Your Live Website, Resumes & LinkedIn Header

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 3. Five points, one free tool, ten minutes — and the one piece of required documentation. Open webaim.org. Run your palette. Get the PDF. That is the entire job. Here is how.

props.segment: The Color Accessibility Check (WebAIM), Step by Step
props.command: Walk me through the WCAG contrast check for my brand palette.
output: WCAG AA: 4.5:1 normal text · 3:1 large text · pass = document it
Deliverable: one PDF with a screenshot of every passing combination

beats.narration_text: First, the vocabulary — said once. WCAG stands for Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, the international standard for accessible web design. The level we target is AA, the minimum required for most professional and academic work. The AA standard sets two thresholds: a contrast ratio of at least 4.5 to 1 for normal text, and at least 3 to 1 for large text — meaning text that is 18 points or larger, or 14 points and bold. The contrast ratio is a purely mathematical comparison of the relative luminance of the text color against the background color. No aesthetic judgment involved. A higher number means more contrast. Black on white is 21 to 1. Light gray on white might be 1.5 to 1 and fail. Your brand colors land somewhere in between, and this check tells you exactly where.

props.artifactHeading: WCAG — what it is and why it matters
artifactLines: WCAG = Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (international standard; use the abbreviation after this).
Level AA = the minimum professional and academic target.
Normal text AA threshold: 4.5:1 contrast ratio (body copy, labels, UI text).
Large text AA threshold: 3:1 (18pt+ regular, or 14pt+ bold).
Contrast ratio = mathematical luminance comparison — not a style opinion.
Black on white = 21:1.  Light gray on white ≈ 1.5:1 (fails).  Your palette is somewhere between.
props.sparkLine: 4.5:1 for normal text. 3:1 for large. AA is the floor.

beats.act: open WebAIM and enter hex codes
beats.narration_text: Open a browser and go to webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker. No login, no account — it is free and instant. You will see two color input fields. The first field is for the foreground color — that is your text color. The second field is for the background color — that is the page or element it sits on. Enter each color as a hex code: the six-character code from your brand sheet, like hashtag-1A2B3C. Type it in or paste it. The tool updates the result the moment you finish typing.

shot.new_visual_element: WebAIM contrast checker — illustrated input flow showing hex entry and result reading

steps: 2. Foreground field → paste your text color hex (e.g. #1A2B3C)
3. Background field → paste your background hex (e.g. #FAF9F5)
4. Result updates instantly — no login, no cost

beats.narration_text: After you enter both hex codes, WebAIM shows you four rows: Normal Text at level AA, Large Text at level AA, Normal Text at level AAA, and Large Text at level AAA. Each row shows either a green PASS badge or a red FAIL badge. For this assignment, the target is AA — the first two rows. If Normal Text passes AA, meaning your ratio is 4.5 to 1 or higher, take a screenshot of that result immediately. The screenshot must be visible and legible — the hex codes in the input fields, the ratio number, and the PASS badge all in the same frame. That screenshot is your evidence.

artifactLines: WebAIM shows four rows: Normal Text AA · Large Text AA · Normal Text AAA · Large Text AAA.
Target for Assignment 8: Normal Text AA (4.5:1 minimum) and Large Text AA (3:1 minimum).
Green badge = PASS. Red badge = FAIL.
Screenshot requirement: hex inputs + ratio number + PASS badge all visible in one frame.
AAA rows are bonus context — not required for the assignment.
props.sparkLine: Normal Text AA: 4.5:1+. Screenshot the PASS.

beats.act: when it fails — how to fix with Claude
beats.narration_text: If your ratio comes back below 4.5 to 1 for normal text, do not start guessing hex codes. Guessing is slow and you will probably land on something that passes but looks wrong. Instead, give Claude the context it needs and ask for three compliant alternatives. Here is the exact prompt. Paste this in verbatim, filling in your own values.

props.command: My brand palette: text color [HEX], background [HEX]. The WebAIM contrast checker shows [RATIO] — it fails WCAG AA (needs 4.5:1 for normal text). Give me three alternative text colors that (1) pass AA, (2) stay within a close family of [HEX], and (3) are still legible on [BACKGROUND_HEX]. Show me the hex and the predicted ratio for each.
output: Option A: #1C3050 — predicted ratio 6.2:1 — PASS AA
Option B: #14294A — predicted ratio 7.1:1 — PASS AA
Option C: #0F2040 — predicted ratio 8.3:1 — PASS AA

beats.act: repeat for all combos and export PDF
beats.narration_text: One passing screenshot is not enough. You need to run every meaningful text-on-background combination in your palette. That means: heading color on page background, body text color on page background, button text on button background, and link color on page background. Run each combination through WebAIM separately. Screenshot each one that passes. When you have all your screenshots, open any free PDF tool — Canva, Google Slides, or any online image-to-PDF converter. Drop all your screenshots in, put them on separate pages or tiles so they are all readable, and export as a single PDF. That PDF is the deliverable for this section of Assignment 8.

props.artifactHeading: Full checklist — text-on-background combos to run
artifactLines: Combo 1: Heading color on page background → screenshot if PASS
Combo 2: Body text color on page background → screenshot if PASS
Combo 3: Button text color on button background → screenshot if PASS
Combo 4: Link color on page background → screenshot if PASS
Collect all passing screenshots → PDF tool (Canva / Google Slides / online converter)
Export: one PDF, all combos visible → that PDF is the deliverable

beats.narration_text: The accessibility check, recapped. One tool, one URL: webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker. The floor for normal text is 4.5 to 1 — that is the AA standard for Web Content Accessibility Guidelines. Large text needs 3 to 1. When something fails, skip the guessing — paste the compliant-alternatives prompt into Claude and pick from three options that are already verified. Screenshot every combination that passes: hex codes, ratio, and badge all visible. One PDF, every combination. That PDF is your submission.

props.artifactTitle: The Color Accessibility Check (WebAIM), Step by Step
artifactLines: One tool, one URL: webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker — free, no login.
Floor: 4.5:1 for normal text (AA); 3:1 for large text (18pt+ or 14pt+ bold).
When it fails: don't guess — paste the compliant-alternatives prompt into Claude.
Screenshot each passing combo: hex codes + ratio + PASS badge, all in one frame.
Deliverable: one PDF containing every text-on-background combination you checked.

beats.narration_text: Your Turn. Open webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker right now. Pull your primary text color and your primary background color from your brand sheet. Enter both hex codes. Read the ratio. If it passes 4.5 to 1, screenshot it. If it fails, paste the prompt below into Claude — fill in your hex codes and your actual ratio — and pick one of the three compliant alternatives it returns. Then run the rest of your combinations and assemble the PDF. Here is the full fix prompt, paste-ready.

props.segment: The Color Accessibility Check (WebAIM), Step by Step
props.command: My brand palette: text color [HEX], background [HEX]. The WebAIM contrast checker shows [RATIO] — it fails WCAG AA (needs 4.5:1 for normal text). Give me three alternative text colors that (1) pass AA, (2) stay within a close family of [HEX], and (3) are still legible on [BACKGROUND_HEX]. Show me the hex and the predicted ratio for each.
props.runningText: paste this into Claude if your ratio fails — then run all combos and build the PDF…

beats.narration_text: The Color Accessibility Check, step by step. Liam, in for Bear.
